entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: Sorghum bicolor (L.) Moench, a crop studied for heat tolerance at the seedling establishment stage

entities.name: PEARL MILLET
entities.description: Pennisetum glaucum (L.) R.Br., a crop studied for heat tolerance at the seedling establishment stage

entities.name: HEAT SHOCK PROTEINS
entities.description: Proteins produced by plants in response to heat stress, characterized by their molecular weight

entities.name: HEAT TOLERANCE
entities.description: The ability of plant cultivars to withstand high temperature stress

entities.name: SEEDLING ESTABLISHMENT
entities.description: The characteristics and success of a plant's early growth stage

entities.name: HEAT STRESS
entities.description: Environmental condition of elevated temperature that affects plant physiology

entities.name: INCUBATION TEMPERATURE
entities.description: The specific temperature range from 35 to 45°C used to induce heat shock proteins in seedlings

relationships.description: Sorghum cultivars exhibit differing levels of heat tolerance
Pearl millet cultivars exhibit differing levels of heat tolerance
Sorghum produces heat shock proteins of 65 and 62 kDa
Pearl millet produces heat shock proteins ranging from 30 to 70 kDa
Heat stress induces the production of heat shock proteins
Heat stress affects seedling establishment characteristics
Alteration of incubation temperature triggers the production of heat shock proteins
Sorghum genotypes differ in seedling establishment characteristics
Pearl millet genotypes differ in seedling establishment characteristics
